🌆 Market News Digest  
April 28, 2026 EST

🔥 Top Stories  
• UAE announces exit from OPEC and OPEC+ — strategic move to boost energy flexibility amid global market shifts.  
• US markets mixed; S&P down 0.5%, Dow up slightly — investor caution ahead of macro and geopolitical updates.  
• Iran reports no nuclear negotiations; claims war is ongoing — escalating tensions and regional instability.  
• China poised to restart fuel exports; easing global energy shortages — potential relief for Asian and European markets.  
• US-Iran Strait of Hormuz tensions persist; vessels rerouted, sanctions tightened — energy supply risks remain high.

⛽ Oil & Energy  
• UAE to gradually increase oil production, exit from OPEC to align with long-term strategy.  
• Brent crude settles at $111.26, up $3.03; WTI at $99.93, up $3.56.  
• US gasoline prices hit $4.18/gal — highest in 4 years amid Iran-US deadlock.  
• Iran claims no nuclear talks; continues oil production, storage for 2 months.  
• China to resume fuel exports in May, easing global shortages.

📊 Markets & Macro  
• US equity futures mixed; S&P futures down 0.48%, Nasdaq down 1%.  
• US consumer confidence at 92.8, beating expectations; inflation expectations tick down.  
• European markets cautious; ECB likely to hold rates amid Iran war impact.  
• US 7-year treasury yield at 4.17%; strong demand at $44B auction.  
• Global GDP forecast for 2026 raised to 2.9%, inflation outlooks revised upward.

🌍 Geopolitical  
• Russia warns of unpredictable consequences over drone deliveries to Kyiv.  
• Gulf leaders meet in Jeddah to discuss Iran conflict escalation.  
• UK and US leaders reaffirm strong alliance; UK’s King Charles addresses Congress.  
• Iran’s military asserts ongoing war; rejects UN Sea Treaty, claims Strait control.  
• US and allies monitor Iranian vessel activity; sanctions on Iranian entities intensify.

🪙 Crypto & DeFi  
• Bitcoin stalls below $76,000 amid US-Iran tensions; market sentiment cautious.  
• Crypto fear & greed index at 33/100 — fear persists.  
• US regulators to use AI for crypto oversight; review of registration applications underway.  
• Crypto trading data under US surveillance; potential crackdown on shadow banking.  
• Iran’s oil and shipping activity monitored via satellite; crypto markets react to geopolitical risks.

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy  
• US Treasury to restrict transactions with Chinese “teapot” refineries linked to Iran.  
• UK Parliament votes against inquiry into PM’s alleged misleading; political stability remains fragile.  
• US SEC and CFTC ramp up crypto regulation; AI tools for oversight introduced.  
• EU to maintain current interest rates; digital markets act to avoid trade barriers.  
• Canada’s debt-to-GDP ratio projected at 41.1% in 2025/26, fiscal outlook cautious.

🏢 Corporate  
• Coca-Cola Q1 earnings beat estimates; revenue $12.5B, EPS $0.86.  
• Airbus reports Q1 revenue €12.65B; adjusted EBIT €300M; guidance unchanged.  
• Visa Q2 adjusted EPS $3.31; revenue $11.23B; resilient consumer spending.  
• Starbucks Q2 revenue $9.5B; EPS $0.50; strong global sales growth.  
• Robinhood crashes 7% after hours post-earnings; investor reaction mixed.

THE OFFER, DELIVERED BY FOREIGN MINISTER ABBAS ARAGHCHI, AIMS TO RESTART STALLED TALKS BUT PUSHES NUCLEAR NEGOTIATIONS TO A LATER STAGE. WASHINGTON HAS NOT RESPONDED.

THE PLAN OUTLINES A PHASED APPROACH: END THE CONFLICT FIRST, THEN ADDRESS CONTROL OF THE STRAIT, AND ONLY AFTERWARD DISCUSS IRAN’S NUCLEAR PROGRAM. ...
